blockers.title: General reverse-flow and QEC transform closure

upstream.subcase: anticommuting reset, measurement, and measure-reset rejection

benchmark.value: Deterministic rejection is not a throughput path and needs no benchmark.

cases.resource_contract: Reject during bounded reverse propagation without constructing an expanded circuit.

upstream.subcase: single Z measurement flow reversal

cases.resource_contract: Use memory proportional to the circuit, flow count, and active Pauli support.

upstream.subcase: four independent MZZ flow relationships

cases.resource_contract: Use memory proportional to target count and flow support without circuit-pattern dispatch.

upstream.subcase: flow support extends to qubit 300 beyond circuit support

cases.resource_contract: Allocate sparse flow state by touched qubits instead of every intermediate qubit id.

upstream.subcase: empty circuit and empty flows

benchmark.value: The empty semantic identity case is too small to be a useful benchmark.

cases.resource_contract: Return empty results without allocating qubit, measurement, or observable state.

upstream.subcase: R, H, MX, and DETECTOR canonical reversal

cases.resource_contract: Preserve detector records with memory linear in operations and active record lookback.

upstream.subcase: M with Z to record flow becomes R

cases.resource_contract: Rewrite one measurement record without scanning unrelated measurement history.

upstream.subcase: R with preparation flow becomes M

cases.resource_contract: Rewrite reset state in one pass with bounded flow-state memory.

upstream.subcase: M with output Pauli and record dependency remains M

cases.resource_contract: Track record parity once without duplicating measurement buffers.

upstream.subcase: dont_turn_measurements_into_resets option

cases.resource_contract: Option handling must not add a second circuit traversal or expanded copy.

upstream.subcase: MR probability becomes trailing X_ERROR

cases.resource_contract: Move noise metadata without sampling or expanding target groups.

upstream.subcase: distance-3 rounds-2 rotated memory-X detecting-region reversal

cases.resource_contract: Use memory linear in generated circuit size and active regions, with no repeat-count expansion.

upstream.subcase: flows touch qubits 300 and 301 around H on qubit 2

cases.resource_contract: Represent high sparse qubit ids without dense allocation through the maximum id.

upstream.subcase: two-target M record order

cases.resource_contract: Preserve target and record order in a single reverse traversal.

upstream.subcase: two-product MZZ record order

cases.resource_contract: Process pair-measurement groups without materializing decomposed circuits.

upstream.subcase: two-target MR record and reset flow order

cases.resource_contract: Track measurement and reset state per target without quadratic flow scans.

upstream.subcase: measurement-record CX feedback remains rejected

benchmark.value: A deterministic unsupported-feedback rejection is not performance-sensitive.

cases.resource_contract: Reject feedback before building transformed circuit output.

upstream.subcase: tagged X and Y observable includes with record conversion

cases.resource_contract: Track observable parity sparsely and preserve tags without cloning unrelated instructions.

upstream.subcase: MPAD constants, records, observables, duplicate ids, and adjacent Clifford matrix

cases.resource_contract: Use memory linear in MPAD targets, flow count, and distinct observable ids.

upstream.subcase: CNOT sweep control plus selected CY, CZ, XCZ, YCZ, and classical CZ matrix

cases.resource_contract: Analyze sweep controls in one reverse pass with no state proportional to sweep index magnitude.

cases.surface: sampler, detection converter, detection sampler, analyzer, and flows

upstream.subcase: all canonical fixed-tableau gates and legal plain-qubit target roles

cases.resource_contract: Dispatch by gate family with no per-gate heap allocation in execution loops.

cases.surface: sampler, detection converter, detection sampler, analyzer, and flows

upstream.subcase: M, MX, MY, R, RX, RY, MR, MRX, and MRY target roles

cases.resource_contract: Reuse per-shot measurement buffers and avoid allocation per target group.

cases.surface: sampler, detection converter, detection sampler, analyzer, and flows

upstream.subcase: MXX, MYY, and MZZ grouped target roles

cases.resource_contract: Process pair groups in bounded scratch proportional to one group.

upstream.subcase: deterministic four-body Pauli-product measurement records

cases.resource_contract: Bound deterministic product reduction scratch by target count and process one target group at a time.

upstream.subcase: anti-Hermitian same-qubit Pauli products reject during semantic execution

benchmark.value: Negative Pauli-product shape rejection is admission behavior, not a throughput workload.

cases.resource_contract: Reject the first anti-Hermitian product during bounded target-group validation without execution or target-count-proportional auxiliary state.

upstream.subcase: deterministic zero and one measurement-pad records

cases.resource_contract: Append deterministic pad records in one pass with reusable per-shot measurement storage.

upstream.subcase: MPP(p) flips deterministic Pauli-product measurement records at the declared probability

cases.resource_contract: Reuse stochastic sampling buffers and keep scratch proportional to one Pauli-product group rather than shot count.

upstream.subcase: MPAD(p) reserves noisy measurement-record space and applies its declared flip probability; pinned Stim has no direct stochastic MPAD GTest

cases.resource_contract: Reuse stochastic measurement-pad buffers and keep scratch proportional to one pad group rather than shot count.

cases.surface: sampler, detection converter, detector frame, analyzer, and flows

upstream.subcase: Hermitian products, multiple groups, inversions, and anti-Hermitian rejection

cases.resource_contract: Lower one product at a time without materializing a full decomposed circuit.

upstream.subcase: X_ERROR, Y_ERROR, and Z_ERROR probability and target roles

benchmark.value: Retain representative report-only gate benchmarks; add a mixed-contract row whenever final PFM-B2 makes production compile or execution dispatch consult the contract.

cases.resource_contract: Sample noise in place with constant scratch per target.

upstream.subcase: PAULI_CHANNEL_1 and PAULI_CHANNEL_2 valid probability tuples

benchmark.value: Retain representative report-only gate benchmarks; add a mixed-contract row whenever final PFM-B2 makes production compile or execution dispatch consult the contract.

cases.resource_contract: Sample channels without allocations proportional to shot count.

upstream.subcase: I_ERROR and II_ERROR execute as exact semantic no-ops

benchmark.value: Identity-noise gates are exact no-ops covered inside representative execution rows; a standalone no-op microbenchmark would not represent user work.

cases.resource_contract: Do not allocate or mutate simulator, converter, analyzer, or flow state for identity-noise targets.

upstream.subcase: DEPOLARIZE1 and DEPOLARIZE2 target roles

benchmark.value: Retain representative report-only gate benchmarks; add a mixed-contract row whenever final PFM-B2 makes production compile or execution dispatch consult the contract.

cases.resource_contract: Use constant scratch for each one- or two-qubit group.

upstream.subcase: CORRELATED_ERROR and ELSE_CORRELATED_ERROR block semantics

benchmark.value: Retain representative report-only gate benchmarks; add a mixed-contract row whenever final PFM-B2 makes production compile or execution dispatch consult the contract.

cases.resource_contract: Track one active correlated-error branch per shot without growing history.

cases.surface: sampler, detection converter, detection sampler, analyzer, and flows

upstream.subcase: HERALDED_ERASE and HERALDED_PAULI_CHANNEL_1 records and errors

benchmark.value: Retain representative report-only gate benchmarks; add a mixed-contract row whenever final PFM-B2 makes production compile or execution dispatch consult the contract.

cases.resource_contract: Append herald records into reused shot buffers without full-output materialization.

cases.surface: sampler, detection converter, analyzer, and flows

upstream.subcase: DETECTOR, OBSERVABLE_INCLUDE, TICK, QUBIT_COORDS, and SHIFT_COORDS

benchmark.value: Annotation dispatch is covered inside representative execution benchmarks.

cases.resource_contract: Process annotations in one pass with sparse detector and observable state.

cases.surface: reference sampler, detection converter, detection sampler, analyzer, and flows

upstream.subcase: record and sweep target roles for CX, CY, CZ, XCZ, and YCZ

cases.resource_contract: Resolve control bits from bounded record lookback or sparse sweep input without dense index allocation.

upstream.subcase: REPEAT is parser-owned control flow and executes through circuit structure instead of regular instruction dispatch

benchmark.value: Repeat execution already has dedicated sampler and folded-consumer benchmarks; this shard proves dispatch ownership instead of adding a duplicate microbenchmark.

cases.resource_contract: Keep repeat execution and folding at circuit-item level without materializing bounded-output-independent repeat counts.

blockers.title: Shared folded DEM traversal and coordinate handling

upstream.subcase: nested repeat detector counting with shifts

cases.resource_contract: Run with scalar summaries proportional to compact model structure and without coordinate-vector state unless a coordinate API requests it.

upstream.subcase: selected coordinate lookup across nested detector and coordinate shifts

cases.resource_contract: Selected lookup must scale with declarations and algebraic candidates visited, exact expanded declaration cardinality overflow must not block sparse lookup, aggregate coordinate scalar work is capped at 8000000 updates, and fractional coordinates use absolute tolerance 1e-12 against pinned Stim accumulation. Full output scales with returned detector ids.

upstream.subcase: repeat-preserving probability rounding

cases.resource_contract: Preserve compact repeats and allocate only the transformed model body.

upstream.subcase: three pairwise detector error combinations plus repeated joint detector-observable parity

cases.resource_contract: Compile in memory bounded by compact model body plus reusable shot state.

upstream.subcase: surface-code detector error model logical-error search

cases.resource_contract: Separate compact traversal memory from explicitly capped exponential search state.

cases.surface: shortest and likeliest error WCNF encoders

upstream.subcase: literal pinned single-detector single-observable unweighted and weighted WCNF encoding plus folded-repeat equivalence

cases.resource_contract: Traverse compact input without expansion while allowing output clauses proportional to encoded mechanisms.

upstream.subcase: surface-code detector error model filter selection

cases.resource_contract: Filter in memory proportional to compact body and selected keys without provenance expansion.

upstream.subcase: H, CX, MXX, detector, and tick-indexed region map

cases.resource_contract: Use bounded reverse region state proportional to requested targets and tick snapshots.

upstream.subcase: implicit-start anticommutation rejection

benchmark.value: A deterministic anticommutation rejection is not a throughput workload.

cases.resource_contract: Reject at the boundary without retaining expanded circuit history.

upstream.subcase: empty circuit returns empty suffix

cases.resource_contract: Return empty output without allocating tracker rows.

upstream.subcase: reset measurement already covered by detector

cases.resource_contract: Track only active independent measurement rows.

upstream.subcase: duplicate detector rows remain covered

cases.resource_contract: Deduplicate parity rows without retaining duplicate dense matrices.

upstream.subcase: reset measurement gains one detector

cases.resource_contract: Produce suffix rows proportional to missing independent stabilizers.

upstream.subcase: bare measurement under known-input semantics

cases.resource_contract: Use one sparse tracker row per independent known-input constraint.

upstream.subcase: bare measurement ignored under unknown-input semantics

cases.resource_contract: Discard unknown-input rows immediately instead of accumulating unresolved state.

upstream.subcase: two measurements with one detector produce one missing row

cases.resource_contract: Reduce multi-record rows by sparse GF(2) elimination.

upstream.subcase: repeated MPP leaves one independent product detector

cases.resource_contract: Represent MPP stabilizers sparsely by touched qubits and measurement records.

upstream.subcase: extra dependent MPP detector changes canonical suffix

cases.resource_contract: Canonicalize dependent rows without enumerating stabilizer subsets.

upstream.subcase: repeated MPP is covered under unknown-input semantics

cases.resource_contract: Unknown-input elimination remains bounded by active sparse rows.

upstream.subcase: record-only observable constraints close MPP rows

cases.resource_contract: Track observable parity sparsely without adding output rows for observables.

upstream.subcase: Pauli observable terms leave one source-owned detector suffix

cases.resource_contract: Apply observable Pauli terms through sparse stabilizer state.

upstream.subcase: 54-qubit honeycomb generated-code suffix

cases.resource_contract: Use sparse row reduction bounded by circuit stabilizer rank and measurement count.

upstream.subcase: 16-qubit toric global stabilizer suffix

cases.resource_contract: Use sparse row reduction bounded by active toric stabilizer rows.

blockers.title: General flow generation and GF(2) measurement solving

upstream.subcase: empty circuit

cases.resource_contract: Return no rows without allocating tableau or measurement state.

upstream.subcase: single X gate signed generators

cases.resource_contract: Use fixed tableau transitions with memory linear in touched qubits.

upstream.subcase: single H gate generators

cases.resource_contract: Use fixed tableau transitions with memory linear in touched qubits.

upstream.subcase: single M gate generators

cases.resource_contract: Generate measurement rows in one pass with bounded record state.

upstream.subcase: duplicate M target parity

cases.resource_contract: Canonicalize duplicate targets without enumerating target subsets.

upstream.subcase: MXX pair generators

cases.resource_contract: Generate pair rows with scratch proportional to touched qubits.

upstream.subcase: multi-pair MYY generators

cases.resource_contract: Generate pair rows with scratch proportional to touched qubits.

upstream.subcase: multi-pair MZZ generators

cases.resource_contract: Generate pair rows with scratch proportional to touched qubits.

upstream.subcase: multi-pair ISWAP canonical order

cases.resource_contract: Apply fixed two-qubit tableau transitions without decomposed circuit allocation.

upstream.subcase: single S gate generators

cases.resource_contract: Use fixed tableau transitions with memory linear in touched qubits.

upstream.subcase: single S_DAG gate generators

cases.resource_contract: Use fixed tableau transitions with memory linear in touched qubits.

upstream.subcase: SPP Z matches S

cases.resource_contract: Apply Pauli-product transitions without full decomposition materialization.

upstream.subcase: SQRT_X followed by S

cases.resource_contract: Compose transitions in one tableau without intermediate circuits.

upstream.subcase: SPP X and Z target groups

cases.resource_contract: Process product groups independently with reusable scratch.

upstream.subcase: SPP X0*X1 generators

cases.resource_contract: Track only qubits touched by the Pauli product.

upstream.subcase: SPP_DAG Z matches S_DAG

cases.resource_contract: Apply inverse product transitions without full decomposition materialization.

upstream.subcase: M followed by CX record feedback

cases.resource_contract: Resolve feedback from bounded record lookback without retaining shot data.

upstream.subcase: single R generator

cases.resource_contract: Generate reset rows with memory linear in touched qubits.

upstream.subcase: single MR generators

cases.resource_contract: Generate measurement-reset rows in one pass with bounded record state.

upstream.subcase: M followed by XCZ record feedback

cases.resource_contract: Resolve feedback from bounded record lookback without retaining shot data.

upstream.subcase: MPAD 0,1,1,0 signed record generators

cases.resource_contract: Generate one constant row per pad target with no qubit state.

upstream.subcase: M followed by CY record feedback

cases.resource_contract: Resolve feedback from bounded record lookback without retaining shot data.

upstream.subcase: heralded erase, heralded channel, and two MPP products

cases.resource_contract: Generate herald and product rows with memory linear in targets and records.

upstream.subcase: two YY MPP products

cases.resource_contract: Generate product rows without expanding products into primitive gates.

upstream.subcase: generated all-operations flow satisfaction

cases.resource_contract: Bound generator rows by configured cap and circuit qubit plus measurement dimensions.

upstream.subcase: empty circuit and query list

cases.resource_contract: Return empty results without constructing a matrix.

upstream.subcase: MX solved, unsolved, identity, and invalid empty-Pauli queries

cases.resource_contract: Solve by GF(2) rows bounded by qubit and measurement dimensions.

upstream.subcase: ten repetition-code flow queries

cases.resource_contract: Solve the generator matrix polynomially without enumerating record subsets.

upstream.subcase: measured-idle and MXX query batches

cases.resource_contract: Use sparse Pauli columns for idle high-index qubits and product measurements.

upstream.subcase: M, MX, MYY, and MPP multi-target generators

cases.resource_contract: Generate rows in memory proportional to qubits and measurement products.

upstream.subcase: M, MX, MYY, and MPP multi-target solutions

cases.resource_contract: Solve product rows polynomially in generator matrix dimensions.

upstream.subcase: prefer one product measurement over nine singles in either order

cases.resource_contract: Choose deterministic low-weight solutions without enumerating all measurement subsets.

upstream.subcase: supported composed circuit with more than sixteen measurements

cases.resource_contract: Use polynomial GF(2) elimination with memory bounded by matrix rows times packed columns.

blockers.title: Generic analyzer loop folding and finite search closure

upstream.subcase: giant repeat with one loop-carried observable dependency

cases.resource_contract: Detect the loop-carried recurrence with state bounded by the canonical analyzer boundary and compact DEM.

upstream.subcase: giant repeat with period-8 logical-observable recurrence

cases.resource_contract: Discover the period-8 recurrence from canonical state without expanding the giant repeat count.

upstream.subcase: giant repeat with period-127 logical-observable recurrence

cases.resource_contract: Discover the period-127 recurrence from canonical state without expanding the giant repeat count.

upstream.subcase: nested repeat folding and detector shifts

cases.resource_contract: Compose nested summaries without multiplying repeat counts into traversal work.

upstream.subcase: generated repetition-code compact folded DEM

cases.resource_contract: Fold generated rounds with state bounded by one round and recurrence state.

upstream.subcase: gauge detector state remains bounded across rounds

cases.resource_contract: Canonical gauge state must stop growing after reaching its recurrence.

upstream.subcase: billion-round gauge-producing repeat nested inside an outer repeat

benchmark.value: The existing analyzer cycle-folding benchmark covers recurrence throughput; this case is a bounded-output regression.

cases.resource_contract: Analyzer recurrence probes must consume gauge outputs at instruction boundaries and remain bounded across nested repeats.

upstream.subcase: multiple maximum-count repeats whose aggregate diagnostic count exceeds u64

benchmark.value: Diagnostic saturation is a constant-time correctness contract rather than a distinct throughput workload.

cases.resource_contract: Observational diagnostic counters saturate and never change analyzer success or output.

upstream.subcase: accept sixteen detector symptoms

benchmark.value: The sixteen-versus-seventeen symptom boundary is too small to be a useful benchmark.

cases.resource_contract: Local decomposition accepts the inclusive fixed sixteen-bit detector-symptom boundary.

upstream.subcase: reject seventeen detector symptoms

benchmark.value: The seventeen-symptom rejection is a fixed error boundary rather than a throughput workload.

cases.resource_contract: Reject the seventeenth detector symptom before allocating exponential local-decomposition combinations.

upstream.subcase: noisy MPAD detector effect with an inert repeat

benchmark.value: The folded MPAD fix adds one existing per-record error lookup and is covered by analyzer workloads.

cases.resource_contract: Record each noisy MPAD measurement error before reverse tracking consumes its measurement record.

upstream.subcase: nested no-recurrence probe work bound

benchmark.value: The nested no-recurrence case is a hostile-input admission boundary and must terminate with a domain error instead of becoming a timing workload.

cases.resource_contract: Charge one cumulative one-million-unit recurrence budget across nested probe circuits and instructions.

cases.surface: circuit_to_detector_error_model folded recurrence probing through the sparse reverse tracker

upstream.subcase: nested supported-unitary billion-repeat probe work bound

benchmark.value: The nested supported-unitary case is a hostile-input admission boundary and must terminate with a domain error instead of becoming a timing workload.

cases.resource_contract: Route analyzer probes through the shared recurrence budget even when the nested repeat is eligible for the normal supported-unitary fast path.

upstream.subcase: detector and coordinate shifts across folded loops

cases.resource_contract: Compose coordinate shifts with checked arithmetic and constant state per coordinate dimension.

upstream.subcase: incomplete observable dependency rejection

benchmark.value: The deterministic dependency rejection is not a throughput workload.

cases.resource_contract: Reject as soon as incomplete loop-carried dependencies are proven.

upstream.subcase: no logical error rejection

benchmark.value: The empty-result rejection is covered inside direct search benchmarks.

cases.resource_contract: Return no-error status without growing a search frontier.

upstream.subcase: zero-probability source error reports no graphlike errors instead of no errors

benchmark.value: Failure-message classification is not a throughput workload.

cases.resource_contract: Count source error instructions for diagnostics while excluding zero-probability mechanisms from graph construction.

upstream.subcase: variable observable-mask payload admission

benchmark.value: The payload admission boundary protects hostile models and is not a successful search workload.

cases.resource_contract: Preflight at most 65,536 terms per state before cloning and at most 5,000,000 stored terms across map keys predecessors and queued copies.

cases.surface: shared graphlike and hypergraph DEM error traversal

upstream.subcase: aggregate source-target work across individually bounded nonzero errors

benchmark.value: Aggregate hostile-input traversal admission is a rejection boundary instead of a successful throughput workload.

cases.resource_contract: Charge at most 20,000,000 source target occurrences across all expanded nonzero error mechanisms before graph construction.

upstream.subcase: indexed unique-edge and persistent observable-mask admission

benchmark.value: Unique-edge and persistent-mask admission protects hostile graph construction and is not a successful search workload.

cases.resource_contract: Use a collision-checked randomized arena index for edge deduplication and admit at most 5,000,000 unique outward edges and 20,000,000 persistent edge-payload or compact-index terms before installation.

upstream.subcase: distance-one exact logical error

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: distance-two canonical target set

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: distance-three canonical target set

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: generated rotated surface-code result

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: generated repetition-code result

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: observable id L1200 survives search

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: no logical error rejection

benchmark.value: The empty-result rejection is covered inside direct search benchmarks.

cases.resource_contract: Return no-error status without growing a search frontier.

upstream.subcase: zero-probability source error does not report no errors

benchmark.value: Failure-message classification is not a throughput workload.

cases.resource_contract: Count source error instructions for diagnostics while excluding zero-probability mechanisms from graph construction.

upstream.subcase: variable detector and observable set payload admission

benchmark.value: The payload admission boundary protects hostile models and is not a successful search workload.

cases.resource_contract: Preflight symmetric-difference cardinality before cloning with at most 65,536 terms per state and 5,000,000 stored terms overall; cap each source error at 65,536 targets before normalization.

upstream.subcase: indexed unique-edge incidence and persistent observable-mask admission

benchmark.value: Unique-edge and persistent-mask admission protects hostile graph construction and is not a successful search workload.

cases.resource_contract: Use collision-checked randomized arena indexing plus indexed incidence deduplication and transactionally admit at most 5,000,000 unique edges and 20,000,000 persistent edge-payload, compact-index, or adjacency terms before installation.

upstream.subcase: distance-one exact logical error

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: distance-two canonical target set

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: distance-three canonical target set

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap.

upstream.subcase: bounded non-graphlike hyperedge result

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap plus the public search frontier limits.

upstream.subcase: generated rotated surface-code result

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap plus the public search frontier limits.

upstream.subcase: generated repetition-code result

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap plus the public search frontier limits.

upstream.subcase: observable id L1200 survives hypergraph search

cases.resource_contract: Allocate only touched detector nodes and compressed observable slots; enforce the independent 5,000,000 expanded nonzero-error-mechanism budget and 1,000,000 effective-detector-node cap plus the public search frontier limits.

upstream.subcase: logical-error-free model

cases.resource_contract: Encode clauses proportional to concrete mechanisms and parity constraints.

upstream.subcase: single observable with no detectors

cases.resource_contract: Encode sparse observable ids without dense allocation.

upstream.subcase: single detector with no observables

cases.resource_contract: Encode sparse detector parity without scanning undeclared ids.

upstream.subcase: large shifted detector-only repeat is trivially unsatisfiable

benchmark.value: The count-summary UNSAT fast path is constant-time metadata work rather than a materialized encoding workload.

cases.resource_contract: Return the fixed UNSAT encoding from compact error and observable counts before traversing or flattening shifted repeats.

upstream.subcase: errors with no detector or observable targets

cases.resource_contract: Skip vacuous mechanisms before allocating SAT variables.

upstream.subcase: empty detector error model

benchmark.value: The empty encoder result is too small to be a useful benchmark.

cases.resource_contract: Return the empty encoding without allocating variable tables.

upstream.subcase: single detector and observable weighted structure

cases.resource_contract: Encode variables and clauses linearly in error mechanisms and parity terms.

upstream.subcase: logical-error-free weighted model

cases.resource_contract: Compute weights once per mechanism without repeat materialization.

upstream.subcase: ordinary probability weighted clauses

cases.resource_contract: Encode weighted clauses linearly in mechanisms and parity terms.

upstream.subcase: probability above one half weight orientation

cases.resource_contract: Normalize weights in constant space per mechanism.

upstream.subcase: half-probability neutral weight

cases.resource_contract: Elide neutral weights without changing parity constraints.

upstream.subcase: stored zero-weight clause remains counted in the WDIMACS header

benchmark.value: Quantization-header compatibility changes encoding text but not encoder complexity.

cases.resource_contract: Preflight and store the clause shape once while preserving Stim's stored-clause header count when zero quantization omits the printed line.

upstream.subcase: large-period unitary loop summary

cases.resource_contract: Skip repeated states with memory bounded by one canonical period.

upstream.subcase: record and detector shifted-copy recurrence

cases.resource_contract: Represent shifts arithmetically without expanding repeated copies.

upstream.subcase: DEM terms, locations, Pauli products, and observables

benchmark.value: Value-object canonicalization is not on the selected measured throughput path.

cases.resource_contract: Sort only active error terms and locations with memory proportional to returned provenance.
